void mprog_wordlist_check(const String &.brief: Checks a character's mobile programs for keyword or phrase triggers within a given argument string and executes matching scripts.
void mprog_wordlist_check(const String &.details:
Checks a list of trigger words against input arguments and executes associated mobile programs if matches are found.
This function iterates through all mobile programs associated with the specified 'mob' character, filtering by the specified trigger 'type'. For each relevant program, it converts the program's argument list and the input argument string to lowercase for case-insensitive matching. If the argument list starts with 'p ', it searches for the presence of the entire phrase within the input string, ensuring word boundaries are respected. Otherwise, it tokenizes the argument list into individual words and searches for each within the input string, again respecting word boundaries. When a match is found, it invokes 'mprog_driver' to execute the associated command list. The function terminates early if the 'mob' object is marked as garbage, preventing further processing.

params.actor: Pointer to the character who initiated the trigger, if applicable.
params.mob: Pointer to the character (mobile) whose triggers are being evaluated.
params.arg: The input argument string to be checked against trigger conditions.
params.vo: Void pointer to a secondary target, such as a character or object, involved in the trigger.
params.type: Bit flag indicating the type of trigger to match against (e.g., speech, command).
params.obj: Pointer to the object associated with the trigger, if any.

void mprog_wordlist_check(const String &.returns: Void; the function performs trigger matching and executes associated scripts without returning a value.
void mprog_wordlist_check(const String &.name: mprog_wordlist_check
void mprog_wordlist_check(const String &.argsstring: (const String &arg, Character *mob, Character *actor, Object *object, void *vo, Flags::Bit type)

void mprog_percent_check(Character *mob,.brief: Checks and executes mobile program commands based on a probabilistic trigger.
void mprog_percent_check(Character *mob,.details:
Performs a probabilistic check to trigger appropriate mob programs based on a random percentile.
This function iterates through the list of mobile program scripts associated with a given mobile entity (mob). For each script, it verifies whether the object is marked as garbage; if so, it terminates early. It then compares the script's type with the specified type parameter and performs a percentage roll using number_percent(). If the roll is below the threshold specified by the script's argument list (converted to an integer), it executes the script's command list via mprog_driver, passing relevant context objects. For certain program types (GREET_PROG and ALL_GREET_PROG), it continues checking subsequent scripts; otherwise, it stops after executing one matching script.

params.actor: Pointer to the Character object representing the actor involved in the trigger.
params.mob: Pointer to the Character object representing the mobile (mob) to evaluate.
params.obj: Pointer to the Object involved in the trigger, if any.
params.vo: Void pointer to additional data passed to the program driver.
params.type: Flags::Bit value indicating the type of mob program to check against.

void mprog_percent_check(Character *mob,.returns: Void; the function performs side effects by executing mob programs when conditions are met.
void mprog_percent_check(Character *mob,.name: mprog_percent_check

void mprog_greet_trigger(Character *ch).brief: Triggers greeting mob programs for NPCs in the same room when a character enters.
void mprog_greet_trigger(Character *ch).details: This function iterates through all characters present in the same room as the specified character 'ch'. For each NPC (non-player character) that is not the character itself, it checks if the NPC can see 'ch', is not currently fighting, and is awake. If these conditions are met, and the NPC has the 'GREET_PROG' or 'ALL_GREET_PROG' program type flags set, it performs a probabilistic check to potentially trigger the corresponding mob program. The function terminates early if the 'ch' object is marked as garbage. It effectively facilitates NPCs greeting or acknowledging the presence of a character entering their vicinity.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character object representing the character who has entered the room and triggers the greeting checks.

void mprog_greet_trigger(Character *ch).returns: Void; the function performs side effects by potentially executing greeting scripts on NPCs in the room.
void mprog_greet_trigger(Character *ch).name: mprog_greet_trigger

void mprog_act_trigger(const char *buf,.brief: Triggers an act event for a mobile entity if it is an NPC with the appropriate program type flags set.
void mprog_act_trigger(const char *buf,.details: This function initiates an action trigger for a specified mobile character ('mob') within the game. If the 'mob' is a non-player character (NPC) and has the 'ACT_PROG' flag set in its program type flags, it creates a new MobProgActList node, links it to the beginning of the 'mob's' action list, and populates it with the provided context information: message buffer ('buf'), character ('ch'), object ('obj'), and generic value ('vo'). This effectively queues an action trigger for the 'mob' to process later. The function does not return a value but modifies the 'mob's' internal action list if conditions are met.

params.buf: A message or command string associated with the action trigger.
params.mob: The target character (mob) to which the action trigger is applied.
params.ch: The character involved in the action, often the actor or source.
params.obj: An object involved in the action, if any.
params.vo: A generic pointer to additional context or data related to the action.

void mprog_act_trigger(const char *buf,.returns: Void; the function modifies the 'mob's' action list if conditions are satisfied.
void mprog_act_trigger(const char *buf,.name: mprog_act_trigger

void mprog_bribe_trigger(Character *mob,.brief: Handles the bribe trigger event for a mobile character, executing associated scripts if conditions are met.
void mprog_bribe_trigger(Character *mob,.details: This function processes a bribe event initiated by a character 'ch' towards a mobile character 'mob'. It first verifies that 'mob' is an NPC; if not, the function exits. It then iterates through the 'mob's' list of mobile program triggers to find a 'BRIBE_PROG' type trigger. Upon finding such a trigger, it creates a money object representing the bribe amount, deducts this amount from the 'mob's' currency, and compares the amount to a threshold specified in the trigger's argument list. If the amount meets or exceeds this threshold, it executes the associated script commands via 'mprog_driver'.

params.mob: The mobile character (NPC) being bribed, which may trigger scripts based on the bribe amount.
params.ch: The character who is offering the bribe.

void mprog_bribe_trigger(Character *mob,.returns: Void; the function performs side effects such as creating money objects, deducting costs, and potentially executing scripts, but does not return a value.
void mprog_bribe_trigger(Character *mob,.name: mprog_bribe_trigger

void mprog_entry_trigger(Character *mob).brief: Triggers entry-related mobile programs for a specified character if conditions are met.
void mprog_entry_trigger(Character *mob).details: This function initiates the execution of entry-type mobile programs (mob programs) for the given character, typically a mobile entity (mob). It first verifies that the character is an NPC by calling Character::is_npc(). If the character is an NPC and has the ENTRY_PROG flag set in its program type flags, it invokes mprog_percent_check to evaluate and potentially execute the associated mob program. The function passes nullptr for object, mob, and additional data parameters, focusing solely on the character's entry trigger. This mechanism allows for scripted behaviors to be triggered automatically when a mob enters a certain state or location.

params.mob: Pointer to the Character object representing the mobile (mob) for which the entry trigger is to be processed.

void mprog_entry_trigger(Character *mob).returns: Void; the function performs its actions without returning a value.
void mprog_entry_trigger(Character *mob).name: mprog_entry_trigger

void mprog_give_trigger(Character *mob,.brief: Triggers mobile program actions when a character gives an object to a mobile entity.
void mprog_give_trigger(Character *mob,.details: This function activates any 'give' type mobile programs associated with the specified mobile character ('mob') when it receives an object ('obj') from another character ('ch'). It first verifies that the 'mob' is a non-player character (NPC) and has the 'GIVE_PROG' flag set. It then iterates through the 'mob's' list of mobile programs ('mobprogs'), extracting the argument list for each. If a program's type is 'GIVE_PROG' and the object name matches the program's argument list or the special keyword 'all', it executes the program's command list via 'mprog_driver', passing relevant entities. The function stops after triggering the first matching program.

params.mob: Pointer to the mobile character (NPC) that may have a 'give' trigger.
params.ch: Pointer to the character who is giving the object.
params.obj: Pointer to the object being given to the 'mob'.

void mprog_give_trigger(Character *mob,.returns: Void; the function performs side effects by executing matching mobile programs if conditions are met.
void mprog_give_trigger(Character *mob,.name: mprog_give_trigger

void mprog_fight_trigger(Character *mob,.brief: Triggers fight-related mobile programs for a character if conditions are met.
void mprog_fight_trigger(Character *mob,.details: This function checks whether the specified 'mob' character is an NPC and has the 'FIGHT_PROG' flag set in its program type flags. If both conditions are true, it invokes 'mprog_percent_check' to potentially execute fight-related mobile programs based on probabilistic triggers. The function does not return a value and serves as a trigger mechanism within the game's event system.

params.mob: Pointer to the Character object representing the mobile (mob) that may trigger fight programs.
params.ch: Pointer to the Character object representing the target character involved in the fight event.

void mprog_fight_trigger(Character *mob,.returns: Void; the function performs its checks and triggers side effects without returning a value.
void mprog_fight_trigger(Character *mob,.name: mprog_fight_trigger

void mprog_buy_trigger(Character *mob, C.brief: Triggers the buy behavior for a mobile character when certain conditions are met.
void mprog_buy_trigger(Character *mob, C.details: This function checks if the specified mobile character ('mob') is an NPC and has the 'BUY_PROG' program type flag set. If both conditions are true, it executes the 'mprog_percent_check' function to determine whether the buy trigger should activate, passing relevant parameters including the actor ('mob'), the character ('ch'), and the program type. The function facilitates NPCs executing buy-related scripts based on probabilistic checks, enabling dynamic in-game interactions.

params.mob: Pointer to the Character object representing the mobile (NPC) to potentially trigger the buy behavior.
params.ch: Pointer to the Character object representing the player or character involved in the interaction.

void mprog_buy_trigger(Character *mob, C.returns: Void; the function performs its actions without returning a value.
void mprog_buy_trigger(Character *mob, C.name: mprog_buy_trigger

void mprog_hitprcnt_trigger(Character *m.brief: Triggers a mobile program when a character's hit points fall below a specified percentage threshold.
void mprog_hitprcnt_trigger(Character *m.details: This function checks if the given mobile character (mob) is an NPC and has the HITPRCNT_PROG flag set. If so, it iterates through the mob's associated mobile programs (mobprogs) and executes the first program whose type matches HITPRCNT_PROG and whose argument list specifies a hit point percentage threshold. The trigger executes the program's command list with the mob as the actor and the specified character (ch) as the target. The function does not return a value and serves as a trigger point for hit point percentage-based behaviors in the game.

params.mob: The mobile character (NPC) that may trigger the program based on its current hit points.
params.ch: The character involved in the trigger event, typically the one whose hit points are being monitored.

void mprog_hitprcnt_trigger(Character *m.returns: Void; the function performs its actions without returning a value.
void mprog_hitprcnt_trigger(Character *m.name: mprog_hitprcnt_trigger

void mprog_death_trigger(Character *mob).brief: Handles the death trigger for a character, executing associated mobile programs and death effects.
void mprog_death_trigger(Character *mob).details: This function checks if the given character (mob) is a non-player character (NPC) with a death program flag set. If so, it probabilistically executes the death program associated with the death event using mprog_percent_check. Regardless of the program execution, it then triggers the death cry for the character, which may involve messaging or other death-related effects. The function operates as a black box to manage death-related behaviors for NPCs with specific programmed responses.

params.mob: Pointer to the Character object representing the NPC that has died and is being processed for death triggers.

void mprog_death_trigger(Character *mob).returns: Void; the function performs actions but does not return a value.
void mprog_death_trigger(Character *mob).name: mprog_death_trigger

void mprog_random_trigger(Character *mob.brief: Triggers a random mobile program check for the specified character if applicable.
void mprog_random_trigger(Character *mob.details: This function initiates a probabilistic trigger for mobile (mob) scripts associated with the given character. It first verifies whether the character's prototype data has the RAND_PROG flag set in its program type flags. If so, it calls the mprog_percent_check function to evaluate and potentially execute a random mob program based on predefined probabilities. The function operates solely on the provided mob character and does not return a value, serving as a trigger point within the game's event system.

params.mob: Pointer to the Character object representing the mobile entity for which the random trigger is to be processed.

void mprog_random_trigger(Character *mob.returns: Void; the function performs its operation via side effects and does not return a value.
void mprog_random_trigger(Character *mob.name: mprog_random_trigger

void mprog_random_area_trigger(Character.brief: Triggers random area-based mobile programs for a specified character (mob).
void mprog_random_area_trigger(Character.details: This function checks if the given character (mob) has the RAND_AREA_PROG flag set in its program type flags. If so, it gathers all rooms within the same area as the mob that currently have players present. It then temporarily moves the mob to each of these rooms in turn, executing a probabilistic mobile program check (mprog_percent_check) for each room. After processing, the mob is returned to its original room unless it was killed during the process. The function ensures that the set of rooms is cleared at the end to prevent residual data. It operates directly on the mob object and interacts with the game world by moving the mob and triggering scripts based on the presence of players in the area.

params.mob: Pointer to the Character object representing the mobile (mob) for which the random area trigger is executed.

void mprog_random_area_trigger(Character.returns: Void; the function performs its actions without returning a value, modifying the game state as a side effect.
void mprog_random_area_trigger(Character.name: mprog_random_area_trigger

If so, it invokes the mprog_percent_check function to evaluate and potentially execute the associated mobile program commands based on a probabilistic check. The function operates as a black box, ensuring that tick-based behaviors are processed for the specified character during each game tick.

params.mob: Pointer to the Character object representing the mobile (mob) for which the tick trigger is to be processed.

void mprog_tick_trigger(Character *mob).returns: Void; the function performs its operations without returning a value.

If the flag is present, it executes the mprog_percent_check function to evaluate and potentially trigger boot-related mobile programs based on probabilistic conditions. The function serves as a trigger mechanism during the character's initialization or loading process, enabling scripted behaviors associated with the boot event.

void mprog_speech_trigger(const String &.brief: Triggers speech-based mobile programs for NPCs in a room based on input text.
void mprog_speech_trigger(const String &.details: This function processes a speech input string and activates any speech-triggered mobile programs (scripts) associated with NPCs present in the same room as the specified character. It iterates through all characters in the room, checks if each NPC has speech triggers enabled, and invokes the trigger checking function for matching scripts. The function ensures that only valid NPCs are considered and stops processing if the primary character is marked as garbage.

params.txt: The input speech text to be checked against NPC speech triggers.
params.mob: The character who is speaking, whose room's NPCs will be evaluated for triggers.
